xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: Plot No. 113 & 114, KNR House, 3rd and 4th Floor, Kavuri Hills, Phase I, Hyderabad, Telangana-500033
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: Plot No. 113 & 114, KNR House, 3rd and 4th Floor, Kavuri Hills, Phase I,Hyderabad, Telangana-500033
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: We serve a distinguished clientele that includes the National Highways Authority of India, the Government of Telangana, the Irrigation Department, State-Level Transport Corporations, the Ministry of Surface Transport, and the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: KNR Agrotech and Beverages Private Limited
KNRC Holdings and Investments Private Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The construction sector is inherently energy-intensive, contributing significantly to both direct and indirect GHG emissions. Without adequate control, these emissions can lead to legal complications and environmental risks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company has adopted measures to address environmental impacts, including the installation of efficient exhaust systems in machinery and vehicles, along with routine performance assessments. Sprinkler systems have been integrated along aggregate conveyors in crushers to control airborne pollutants. Emissions are regularly monitored to strengthen the overall emissions management framework.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Construction and real estate operations are highly dependent on electricity and other natural resources. Efficient management of this energy use is essential for reducing operational costs and environmental impact.

The environmental efficiency of real estate assets is under increasing scrutiny. Companies are expected to comply with environmental regulations and improve their sustainability credentials to stay competitive.
Climate change introduces both physical (floods, extreme weather) and transitional (regulatory, energy shifts) risks. These can disrupt construction timelines and affect infrastructure integrity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Operational plans are designed to anticipate and adapt to climate variability, minimizing disruptions. Flood-preventive drainage systems are incorporated at work sites, and climate-resilient materials like high-performance bitumen are selected to withstand extreme conditions. Tailored mix designs further enhance the resilience of infrastructure to climatic uncertainties.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Water is essential across various phases of construction. Overuse or improper discharge can deplete or pollute local water sources, triggering cost increases and legal repercussions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Water bodies near construction sites are periodically tested to ensure safety. Sprinkler systems are deployed to optimize water use in soil compaction and dust suppression at crushers and access roads. Recycled water is used in curing processes at casting yards and for landscaping activities. These practices reflect the Company’s long-term commitment to sustainable water management.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Adopting a circular economy approach—reuse, recycle, refurbish—can significantly lower material costs and reduce waste. It also prepares the business for evolving regulatory landscapes.
Infrastructure development can disrupt natural ecosystems. Pre- and post-project monitoring is essential to reduce negative environmental impacts and ensure compliance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: All projects are initiated post-clearance from competent government authorities. Environmental compliance is maintained through mandated mitigation protocols and continuous monitoring of air, water, and soil. Tall barricades and plantations within project sites reduce pollution dispersion. To offset deforestation, multiple trees are planted for every tree removed, and transplantation is considered wherever feasible.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Transitioning to circular economy principles promotes sustainable consumption, resource efficiency, and long-term cost savings.
Employee satisfaction leads to higher retention, while high attrition increases costs and risks. It may also negatively affect the Company's reputation among stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company identifies talent development needs and provides training aligned with project requirements. Clear career advancement paths, employee-friendly policies, and supportive welfare programs foster a stable workforce. Mentorship initiatives and financial stability have contributed to strong retention across functions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: High diversity and inclusion levels enhance organizational culture, promote innovation, and strengthen brand reputation.
Customer satisfaction is a direct indicator of business performance and impacts future contracts and client relationships.
Poor construction quality can result in legal liability, rework costs, and reputational damage. It may also pose safety risks due to changing climatic conditions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: KNRCL enforces stringent QA/QC procedures throughout all projects, including roads, canal systems, and reservoir bunds. The successful completion of Defect Liability Periods and asset monetization under the HAM model serve as indicators of sustained quality assurance and execution excellence.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: A large, decentralized workforce requires robust systems to ensure fair labour practices, worker safety, and compliance with human rights regulations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company manages its diverse workforce in line with government regulations—ensuring safe working environments, onsite accommodations, and fair wages. Dedicated coordinators are deployed at project locations to address workers' basic needs, and deviations are swiftly addressed to uphold human rights standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Construction can bring both development and disruption. Lack of community involvement may lead to resistance, delays, or even project cancellations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: A dedicated Manager is assigned to each project to maintain consistent communication with local communities. Through CSR initiatives like road construction, water access, and support for self-help groups, the Company fosters goodwill. When communities understand the benefits of infrastructure development, cooperation and long-term support significantly improve.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Ethics-related violations like fraud or corruption can lead to investigations, penalties, and reputational loss. Strong governance systems are essential to prevent such risks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company rigorously follows internal financial control guidelines and ensures compliance with all regulatory requirements. The Audit Committee oversees quarterly reporting, promoting transparency and accountability. Strong credit ratings and consistent dividend distribution underscore KNRCL’s commitment to ethical business conduct.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Investors assess companies on their governance practices, which influence accountability, transparency, and long-term value creation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Board holds regular meetings to oversee governance practices, while internal audits support continuous evaluation. Audit findings are reviewed by the Board, signaling the Company’s commitment to integrity and responsible leadership.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Handling sensitive data involves risk of breaches, regulatory action, and reputational damage. Securing data is essential to protect stakeholders and business integrity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: The Company enforces strict data access controls, limiting information access based on roles. Third-party data sharing is prohibited unless specifically approved. These measures ensure robust data security and align with the Company’s commitment to privacy protection.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: Since the ESG reporting is at the initial stage, commitments and targets will be set out and performance of the same will be evaluated in due course of time.
Since the ESG reporting is at the initial stage, commitments and targets will be set out and performance of the same will be evaluated in due course of time.

Since the ESG reporting is at the initial stage, commitments and targets will be set out and performance of the same will be evaluated in due course of time.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT: The Company is in the process of establishing a mechanism to measure progress on performance against the specific commitments, goals and targets.
The Company is in the process of establishing a mechanism to measure progress on performance against the specific commitments, goals and targets.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible:
Dear Stakeholders, As the director responsible for the Business Responsibility Report, I am pleased to present an overview of our ESG initiatives to our esteemed shareholders. Our commitment to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles forms the foundation of our business philosophy.
Environment: We have implemented effective exhaust mechanisms for machinery and vehicles, with regular checks to ensure optimal performance. Sprinklers on conveyors help reduce air emissions from aggregate crushers, supported by consistent emissions monitoring. Our electricity consumption is managed through captive sources like generators. We optimize waste materials from structures and pavements, using them in diversion roads and construction layers. Our focus on waste reduction, reusing, and efficient water use underscores our commitment to environmental sustainability.
Social: Our social initiatives include identifying and training individuals for project deployment, fostering career progression opportunities, and maintaining high employee retention through supportive policies and welfare activities. Our mentoring programs contribute to staff retention and consistent performance.
Governance: Governance is a cornerstone of our operations. Frequent Board meetings ensure accountability, transparency, ethical decision-making, and adherence to business ethics. Internal audits reinforce our commitment to sound governance practices, enhancing shareholder and investor confidence.
In conclusion, our ESG initiatives encompass environmental sustainability, social empowerment, and robust governance practices. We are dedicated to addressing challenges, meeting targets, and fostering responsible growth, guided by our commitment to creating lasting value for all stakeholders. We appreciate your ongoing support and confidence in our organization. Together, we are poised to build a brighter and more sustainable future.
Sincerely, K Jalandhar Reddy Executive Director
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Shri K Jalandhar Reddy, Executive Director
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: Shri K Jalandhar Reddy, Executive Director
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: SAP MM, SAP Fico, SAP Stores, SAP P&M, HR Payroll, Health and Safety program.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: Joint commissioner of Income Tax, Central Circle, Central Range-2, Hyderabad
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: Penalty order passed of Rs.18,22,08,922/- u/s 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act, 1961 for the AY 2007-08.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheCase: Penalty order passed of Rs.18,22,08,922/- u/s 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act, 1961 for the AY 2007-08*
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: Joint Commissioner of Income Tax, Central Circle, Central Range-2, Hyderabad
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: KNRCL has implemented an Anti-Bribery Policy aimed at eliminating any form of bribery or corruption. All individuals connected with the Company, including those from its subsidiaries and affiliates, are expected to fully comply with the guidelines and coverage outlined in this policy.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: The Company’s Code of Conduct mandates that all directors refrain from engaging in activities that could conflict with the Company’s best interests. Additionally, directors are required to annually confirm their compliance with the Code of Conduct to the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText:
1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.2: There were no such cases during the year for Settlement, Compounding fee, Imprisonment, Punishment.
2. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.3: Appeal has been filed
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple2ExplanatoryText: 1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.3: Given the nature of the Company’s operations, this is not applicable.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Employees or workers can raise concerns or lodge grievances with the respective project managers at their sites. The project managers will escalate these concerns or grievances to the Project Director, who will address them within 30 days following a thorough investigation. All grievances received and their status will be reported to the Managing Director or Executive Director in a timely manner.

Employees or workers can raise concerns or lodge grievances with the respective project managers at their sites. The project managers will escalate these concerns or grievances to the Project Director, who will address them within 30 days following a thorough investigation. All grievances received and their status will be reported to the Managing Director or Executive Director in a timely manner.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: KNRCL places utmost importance on the safety and well-being of its employees and all stakeholders. Our Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) Policy reflects our dedication to effectively managing critical HSE elements. We have implemented a Health, Safety, and Environment Management System that is certified under the ISO 45001:2018 standard.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: We assess occupational health and safety risks across all ongoing, new, or modified operations, processes, products, services, and any regulatory updates — covering both routine and non-routine tasks. Our risk assessments are conducted quarterly and include incident reviews. Identified hazards are prioritized for elimination or control. Once control measures are applied as per the hierarchy of controls, we re-evaluate to determine any remaining residual risks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: We are committed to fostering a safety-first culture that promotes individual accountability. Personnel at our project sites receive training to recognize, manage, and control risks specific to their operations. We conduct regular site inspections and health and safety awareness programs. Our established safety systems include work permits, structured training, LOTO (lockout/tagout) protocols, operational controls, monitoring mechanisms, audits, and periodic assessments. Identified gaps, deviations, findings, and lessons learned are addressed through appropriate controls, which are tracked to ensure timely and effective closure. Furthermore, the Company has implemented a WCA policy for workers deployed at project sites.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: During the year under review, there were no safety related incidents occurred. However, the Company has in place certain SOPs for supervising and monitoring the safety practices being followed and the equipment operation, overall safety and health of the employees and workers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText:
1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.5: No parental leave obtained by workers during the year under review
2. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.6: The Company does not have any staff in ‘Other than permanent employees’ and ‘Other than permanent workers category’.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: Identifying stakeholders is a cornerstone of our strategy for achieving business objectives. We follow a structured approach that includes a comprehensive analysis of our operations to pinpoint key stakeholders such as investors, vendors, customers, employees, lenders, and the broader community. Our assessment also considers the influence of our initiatives on the communities we interact with, recognizing their vital role in our engagement efforts. By gaining insight into stakeholder expectations, we proactively address potential risks, anticipate needs, and foster long-term relationships that drive our success. This forward-looking engagement enhances both strategic coherence and overall performance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Financial performance, operations of the Company
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Meetings (Capital equipment – regular basis; Material – as and when required)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Community issues near the project sites
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: The policies and procedures for addressing employee grievances will be implemented with the utmost sensitivity, urgency, and priority in managing and resolving HR grievances.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: The Company has put in place a structured process to handle complaints concerning discrimination and harassment. This process upholds strict confidentiality during investigations and ensures the complainant’s identity remains protected. Moreover, measures are in place to shield the complainant from any retaliatory actions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: The Company is constantly assessing the sites to ensure that there are no instances of child labour, forced labor, sexual harassment, discrimination at work place and inequality in payment of wages. The Company will be taking corrective actions as and when any such instances are reported from the sites
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple5ExplanatoryText: 1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.3: i.Only the Executive Directors remuneration is considered for calculating the median remuneration of the Board of Directors. ii.The executive directors are the existing KMP of the Company and hence it is the same. iii. In male category MD and ED are excluded and in female category CS is excluded.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: All construction vehicles, equipment, and machinery are regularly maintained to meet the pollution emission standards set by the State Pollution Control Board. Additionally, a tree plantation scheme has been implemented along the project highway.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: The Company has implemented waste management methods such as utilising landfills, outsourcing to third-party disposal services, and employing incineration techniques. Being a Construction company, we are not producing any such kind of toxic chemicals. Further, the Company is also exploring various methods/SOPs for waste management process and their disposal in such a way that doesn’t impact the environment adversely.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText:
1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.1: i. The revenue from operations has been adjusted for Purchasing Power Parity (PPP) using the latest PPP conversion factor published by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) for India for the year 24-25, which is 20.66. ii. The Company undertakes different types of projects, which includes construction of highways and irrigation projects. Due to the varying nature of these projects, a standardized unit of measurement is not applicable. Consequently, calculating physical intensity, which typically requires a common unit of measurement, is not feasible in this context.
2. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.3: The Company undertakes different types of projects, which includes construction of highways and irrigation projects. Due to the varying nature of these projects, a standardized unit of measurement is not applicable. Consequently, calculating physical intensity, which typically requires a common unit of measurement, is not feasible in this context.
3. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.6: i. Particulate Matter has been reported separately as PM10 and PM2.5, in line with monitoring protocols and NAAQM standards. Therefore, the general PM row has been marked as Not Applicable (NA). ii. Not applicable as company’s activities do not involve these emissions. iii. For the previous financial year, data is reported as Not Applicable (NA) since the Company had not built its capability to monitor and track air emissions. The monitoring mechanism has been established in the current reporting year.
4. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.7: i. Source of emission factors used - EPA’s GHG Emission Factors Hub, CEA’s CDM - CO2 Baseline Database User Guide Version 20 has been used for the purpose of GHG Emissions calculations. ii. The Company undertakes different types of projects, which includes construction of highways and irrigation projects. Due to the varying nature of these projects, a standardized unit of measurement is not applicable. Consequently, calculating physical intensity, which typically requires a common unit of measurement, is not feasible in this context.
5. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.9: i. During the previous FY 2023-24, projects situated in Kerala, periyasanti and Magadi were with the alignment passing along the existing road and work were in peak progress. Hence the generation of demolition and construction waste was high. For the FY 2024-25, no such activities were required to be carried out and there is hardly any balance work left. The other projects are greenfield projects and construction and demolition waste doesn’t arise. ii. The Company undertakes different types of projects, which includes construction of highways and irrigation projects. Due to the varying nature of these projects, a standardized unit of measurement is not applicable. Consequently, calculating physical intensity, which typically requires a common unit of measurement, is not feasible in this context. iii. Further, the demolition waste such had been reused for laying of new road/service road/ alternate road nearby the project. iv. Battery waste & Plastic waste are given to third party vendor.
6. Note refers to the essential indicator 11: There are no operation in/around ecologically sensitive areas.
7. Note refers to the essential indicator 12: No Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) was undertaken during the financial year
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple7ExplanatoryText: 1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.2: There were no issues raised related to anti-competitive conduct during this financial year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Stakeholders may report concerns or grievances directly to the project managers at the respective sites. These concerns are then escalated to the Project Director, who is responsible for resolving them within 30 days after a detailed investigation. The status of all grievances is promptly communicated to the Managing Director or Executive Director.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText:
1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.1: No Social Impact Assessment (SIA) was undertaken during the financial year.
2. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.2: The company has not undertaken any projects during the financial year that required Rehabilitation and Resettlement (R&R) activities.
3. Note refers to the leadership indicator Q.6: The details of the beneficiaries of free food, construction of library, construction of toilets in school, construction of road in Dammannapet, and contribution towards eye surgeries are not ascertainable as these facilities are made for the benefit of the public at large.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: The Company is engaged in the construction and engineering sector, with a focus on highways, and does not deal in consumer products. Common concerns or grievances usually pertain to construction quality and project timelines. Stakeholders may submit their concerns through mail or letters, which are then forwarded to the respective project heads for prompt resolution. Customers also have the option to email their concerns to info@knrcl.com.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple9ExplanatoryText:
1. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.2: This is not applicable, as our operations do not encompass manufacturing activities.
2. Note refers to the essential indicator Q.6: No such instances were reported during the financial year.
